network_policies.name: etcd
issues: policyTypes does not include Egress (all egress traffic is allowed)

network_policies.name: modelmesh-controller
issues: ingress rule 1 allows traffic from all sources (no 'from' selector)
policyTypes does not include Egress (all egress traffic is allowed)

network_policies.name: modelmesh-runtimes
issues: ingress rule 2 allows traffic from all sources (no 'from' selector)
ingress rule 3 allows traffic from all sources (no 'from' selector)
policyTypes does not include Egress (all egress traffic is allowed)

network_policies.name: modelmesh-webhook
issues: ingress rule 1 allows traffic from all sources (no 'from' selector)
policyTypes does not include Egress (all egress traffic is allowed)
k8s.io modules span multiple minor versions: v0.28 (k8s.io/api, k8s.io/client-go); v0.30 (k8s.io/apimachinery)
No GOMEMLIMIT set in deployment (Go GC cannot pressure-tune). Set GOMEMLIMIT to 80-90% of container memory limit for optimal GC behavior
No cache configuration: all informers are cluster-wide (OOM risk). See https://book.kubebuilder.io/reference/watching-resources/filtering for cache filtering patterns
Type ClusterServingRuntime is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type ConfigMap is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type Deployment is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type InferenceService is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type Namespace is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type Predictor is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type Secret is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type Service is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type ServiceMonitor is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)
Type ServingRuntime is watched but has no cache filter (cluster-wide informer)

containers.name: torchserve
args: while [ ! -e "$TS_CONFIG_FILE" ]; do echo "waiting for config file..."; sleep 1; done;

api_types.name: PredictorSpec
api_types.doc: PredictorSpec defines the desired state of Predictor +k8s:openapi-gen=true

fields.name: ServiceAccountName
fields.doc: NOT YET SUPPORTED

fields.name: Gpu
fields.doc: May be absent, "preferred" or "required"

fields.name: Runtime
fields.doc: If omitted a compatible runtime is selected based on the model type (if available)

fields.name: ProtocolVersion
fields.doc: Protocol version to be exposed by the predictor (i.e. v1 or v2 or grpc-v1 or grpc-v2)

api_types.name: Predictor
api_types.doc: Predictor is the Schema for the predictors API +k8s:openapi-gen=true

api_types.name: PredictorList
api_types.doc: PredictorList contains a list of Predictor +k8s:openapi-gen=true

fields.name: Path
fields.doc: The path to the model object in the storage. It cannot co-exist with the storageURI.

fields.name: SchemaPath
fields.doc: The path to the model schema file in the storage.

fields.name: Parameters
fields.doc: Parameters to override the default storage credentials and config.

fields.name: StorageKey
fields.doc: The Storage Key in the secret for this model.

fields.name: Location
fields.doc: Name of component to which the failure relates (usually Pod name)

fields.name: Reason
fields.doc: High level class of failure

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: Detailed error message

fields.name: ModelId
fields.doc: Internal ID of model, tied to specific Spec contents

fields.name: Time
fields.doc: Time failure occurred or was discovered

api_types.name: PredictorStatus
api_types.doc: PredictorStatus defines the observed state of Predictor +k8s:openapi-gen=true

fields.name: Available
fields.doc: Whether the predictor endpoint is available

fields.name: TransitionStatus
fields.doc: Whether the available predictor endpoint reflects the current Spec or is in transition

fields.name: ActiveModelState
fields.doc: High level state string: Pending, Standby, Loading, Loaded, FailedToLoad

fields.name: LastFailureInfo
fields.doc: Details of last failure, when load of target model is failed or blocked

fields.name: HTTPEndpoint
fields.doc: Addressable endpoint for the deployed trained model This will be "static" and will not change when the model is mutated

fields.name: TotalCopies
fields.doc: Total number of copies of this predictor's models

fields.name: FailedCopies
fields.doc: How many copies of this predictor's models failed to load recently

fields.name: RuntimeRef
fields.doc: one-of these must be present

fields.name: StorageSpec
fields.doc: new way to specify the storage configuration

fields.name: PersistentVolumeClaim
fields.doc: (DEPRECATED) PersistentVolmueClaim was never supported this way and will be removed

fields.name: S3
fields.doc: (DEPRECATED) S3 has configuration to connect to an S3 instance. It is now deprecated, use fields from Spec.Storage instead.

fields.name: Path
fields.doc: (DEPRECATED) The path to the model files within the storage

fields.name: SchemaPath
fields.doc: (DEPRECATED) The path to the schema file within the storage

operator_config.name: DefaultRequeueTime
operator_config.doc: The amount of time failed requests are queued before reattempting

operator_config.name: PullerContainerName
operator_config.doc: The name of the puller container

operator_config.name: PullerEnvListenPort
operator_config.doc: The env variable puller uses to configure it's own listen port

operator_config.name: PullerEnvModelDir
operator_config.doc: The env variable puller uses to configure the models dir

operator_config.name: PullerEnvModelServerEndpoint
operator_config.doc: The env variable puller uses to configure the target model serving port

operator_config.name: PullerEnvPVCDir
operator_config.doc: The env variable puller uses to configure the pvc dir (secrets)

operator_config.name: PullerEnvStorageConfigDir
operator_config.doc: The env variable puller uses to configure the config dir (secrets)

operator_config.name: udsVolMountName
operator_config.doc: uds path volume mount name for puller containers

operator_config.name: noHomeMessage
operator_config.value: There are no running instances that meet the label requirements of type
operator_config.doc:
This is the error message from model-mesh when there are no ready Pods which can load models of
this model's type. Examples of the full message:
"There are no running instances that meet the label requirements of type mt:SomeType: [mt:SomeType]"
"There are no running instances that meet the label requirements of type rt:SomeRuntime: [rt:SomeRuntime]"
"There are no running instances that meet the label requirements of type _default: [_no_runtime]"

operator_config.name: internalVolumes
operator_config.value:
sets.New[string](
	modelmesh.ConfigStorageMount,
	modelmesh.EtcdVolume,
	modelmesh.InternalConfigMapName,
	modelmesh.SocketVolume,
	modelmesh.ModelsDirVolume,
)

summary: modelmesh-serving is a Kubernetes component. managing 4 CRD(s): ClusterServingRuntime, InferenceService, Predictor, ServingRuntime. deploying 3 workload(s) (controller-manager, modelmesh-controller, etcd) with 4 container(s). watching 10 resource type(s). exposing 3 service(s). with 1 webhook(s) (1 validating). protected by 4 network policy/policies. connecting to external services: azure-blob. defining 2 API spec type(s) across 12 struct(s) with 43 total fields. with 93 operator config constants.
